name: The Tower Summit and Iron Tank Chamber
description: The interior upper level of the stone tower, where the iron water tank sits in its limestone housing, the Tank_Hydrostatic_Load gauge mounted on the iron inspection hatch frame — currently reading zero, waiting for the first water.
atmosphericDetails: The structural iron bands of the tank produce a low, resonant moan as the stone around them settles under the evening temperature drop — a sound that carries down through the entire tower.
The view from the summit inspection hatch takes in the entire village grid and, to the east, the faint orange glow of Chicago's gas lighting on the horizon.
The iron tank smells of linseed oil and new metal — it was painted inside three weeks ago and the smell has not fully dissipated.
